finalPrompt: You know now which flight was missing, and why it never left. Say what Moshé chose instead of boarding it — the words that turned every landscape into the stage of their story.

fragments.label: Fragment I
fragments.text: The erased line of the boarding pass was never a destination. It was a return.

fragments.label: Fragment II
fragments.text: The rabbit appeared the first time either of them needed an excuse to keep talking.

fragments.label: Fragment III
fragments.text: Every wrong connection kept two tickets side by side.

fragments.label: Fragment IV
fragments.text: The missing page of the wedding held a blessing about building a home together.

fragments.label: Fragment V
fragments.text: A question asked too early is not wrong. It is only waiting.

fragments.label: Fragment VI
fragments.text: An echo answers with your own words, a little softer, a little truer.

fragments.label: Fragment VII
fragments.text: Gates within gates: each departure was really a way of staying.

fragments.label: Fragment VIII
fragments.text: Seoulmate: the word he built from a city, so she would hear the word inside it.

notebookQuestions.text: Why does Sharon's invitation keep erasing itself?
Why does the white rabbit appear where it should not?
Why was Sharon not waiting in Mumbai?
Who was erased from the photograph?
What is the brass Hamsa for?
Which flight is the missing flight?

clues.visibleForm: Sharon's invitation rewrites itself; the venue line fades first.
clues.trueMeaning: The invitation is a memory-object: it shows only what has already been re-earned by remembering.

clues.visibleForm: The erased boarding-pass block is too short for MUMBAI — it fits a return code.
clues.trueMeaning: The missing flight is not a leg of the outbound route. It is a return flight someone never took.

clues.visibleForm: The direct Paris–Mumbai flight is marked cancelled.
clues.trueMeaning: Red herring: the cancelled direct flight is ordinary weather trouble, not the mystery.

clues.visibleForm: Four clocks disagree — one shows a time from years ago.
clues.trueMeaning: Time anomaly: the journey is being replayed from memory, not lived forward.

clues.visibleForm: A group photograph on the arrivals floor: two figures are blank white outlines.
clues.trueMeaning: The erased figures are Leah and Moshé themselves — you cannot appear in a memory you are still rebuilding.

clues.visibleForm: A brass Hamsa waits under the arrivals sign; its eye is scratched with tiny route lines.
clues.trueMeaning: The Hamsa is Sharon's anchor-gift: it marks which memories are true when anomalies appear.

clues.visibleForm: A note in Sharon's hand: 'Start where you two started.'
clues.trueMeaning: Sharon deliberately is not at arrivals; the journey through memories is her wedding gift and her question.

clues.visibleForm: A white rabbit appeared on the Havdalah table the night Moshé finally spoke to Leah.
clues.trueMeaning: The rabbit marks the moments when staying was chosen over leaving — it began as the excuse for a first conversation.

clues.visibleForm: On the debris hill lies a metro card dated years after that afternoon.
clues.trueMeaning: Object anomaly: memories are being contaminated with later knowledge — proof this is reconstruction.

clues.visibleForm: The footprints on the cold beach arrive but never leave.
clues.trueMeaning: Anomaly rule: every contaminated memory hides one impossible detail of six kinds — time, place, person, direction, object, phrase.

clues.visibleForm: In the SAR-EL group photo, the same two silhouettes are missing that were missing in Mumbai.
clues.trueMeaning: Confirms the erased figures are always Leah and Moshé, across every memory.

clues.visibleForm: Six wrong turns in Mea Shearim traced the shape of a letter no map contains.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: the wrong turns spell the first stroke of the final phrase — getting lost together was the point.

clues.visibleForm: A musician in Athens hums the tune Sharon always hummed.
clues.trueMeaning: Sharon's presence threads the journey; she has been seeding the route with guides.

clues.visibleForm: Old tickets in the travel pouch: every 'wrong connection' in Europe still kept two seats adjacent.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: the itinerary was never optimized for arrival — it was optimized for staying together.

clues.visibleForm: A green suitcase went missing in Istanbul.
clues.trueMeaning: Red herring: the musician took it by accident; ordinary travel chaos, not mystery.

clues.visibleForm: One page of the wedding booklet is missing — the blessing of the home.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: the ceremony cannot finish until the players supply the missing promise themselves.

clues.visibleForm: In the cave, every visitor has a stone double — except two.
clues.trueMeaning: Confirms the person-anomaly: the two without doubles are the two erased from photographs.

clues.visibleForm: A letter fragment in Sharon's hand: 'my gift is the journey back through yours.'
clues.trueMeaning: Sharon's motivation: her wedding gift is forcing Leah and Moshé to relive their story until they answer its open question.

clues.visibleForm: On Koh Phi Phi, Moshé asked an enormous question far too early, and they both laughed.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: the impulsive question was real. The 'missing flight' is the return he was supposed to take alone afterwards.

clues.visibleForm: A ring-shaped mark in Moshé's Paris notebook page.
clues.trueMeaning: Red herring: it is a coffee-cup stain; Paris was never the proposal plan.

clues.visibleForm: The cave echo returns their words changed: 'go' comes back as 'stay'.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: the memory-world itself keeps answering every departure with the same word.

clues.visibleForm: Rabbit Marks tally: the rabbit only ever appears at farewells that didn't happen.
clues.trueMeaning: The rabbit's function: it marks averted goodbyes.

clues.visibleForm: Every gate they photographed in Japan frames the two of them walking in, never out.
clues.trueMeaning: Major clue: direction anomaly resolved — in this reconstruction there are entrances only; no one ever leaves.

clues.visibleForm: Held against an anomaly, the Hamsa's eye stays clear only on true details.
clues.trueMeaning: The Hamsa is the arbiter between authentic clues and red herrings in the finale.

clues.visibleForm: 'You… are… my… Seoulmate' — the wordplay lands, and the last fragment glows.
clues.trueMeaning: Final fragment: the answer to the missing flight is the sentence he said instead of boarding it.

clues.visibleForm: Seen from the second perspective, the rabbit on Phi Phi sat exactly where the return ferry left.
clues.trueMeaning: The rabbit was there when the missing flight's journey home should have begun — and didn't.

clues.visibleForm: A 'proposal photo' at Fuji shows a flower arch and a ring box.
clues.trueMeaning: Red herring: the box held rice snacks; the Hamsa's eye clouds over it.

clues.visibleForm: In the Jerusalem thrift shop they once tried on hypothetical wedding clothes, laughing.
clues.trueMeaning: Independent confirmation that the question asked too early had a true intention behind it.
